steps.title: Notice the parasite paradox
steps.content: Three-host CLPs are usually shielded from external conditions during transmission, while directly transmitted parasites are exposed. Yet CLP abundance decreased as temperatures rose, and directly transmitted parasite abundance was stable.

steps.title: Explain the decline despite shielding
steps.content: Choices A and D reverse the observed pattern, and Choice B introduces transmission differences not discussed. The remaining choice explains that any shielding advantage was outweighed by other temperature-driven effects. The answer is C.
